cases.prompt: Act as the eval-system engineer: walk the TypeScript under `workspace/plugins/zoto-eval-system/engine/`, `workspace/plugins/zoto-eval-system/_user-case-guards/`, and the hook entrypoints, then report duplication, dead exports, error-path quality, and test coverage gaps. Classify severities and size recommendations; do not alter plugin sources unless this message explicitly authorises edits.
assertions: Every substantive finding cites a `startLine:endLine:filepath` span in plain text.
Findings classify impact using the blocker through info ladder spelled out for this agent.
The narrative keeps plugin directories and `.zoto/eval-system/` read-only unless edit authorisation appeared in the user message.
cases.expected_output: A markdown audit where each finding cites a line-range code reference, uses the documented severity bands, avoids edits outside authorised scope, and names the downstream owner only when escalation is warranted.

cases.prompt: From the engineer role: for each schema under `workspace/plugins/zoto-eval-system/templates/schema/`, explain whether TypeScript mirrors stay aligned or drift; cross-check README claims where they collide with schema fields. Trace `preserveUserAuthoredCases` and `writeMetaMarker` through schema, configurer, and updater so refusal happens before manifest reads or writes if `false` arrives. Tie Pattern B observations to concrete subagent files if you inspect any.
assertions: Written output names checks that cover config, manifest, result, case-meta, analyser-payload, cleanup-plan, and needs-user-input schema purposes.
The review verifies `update.preserveUserAuthoredCases` and nested mirrors cannot reach disk when set to forbidden values prior to reads or writes.
Comments on `_meta.generated` partitions reference both case-level YAML markers and banner-style whole-file guards without proposing edits where user-authored cases lack generated markers.

When diagnosing subagent Q&A layering, the text states structured `needs_user_input` payloads are the subagent hand-off shape and warns that askQuestion emissions from inside the delegated subagent loop are contract violations.
If indispensable repository artefacts are unreachable in the briefing, the agent returns structured `needs_user_input` instead of emitting askQuestion from the subagent loop.
cases.expected_output: A structured review spanning all seven documented schemas versus code and prose, confirming early refusal semantics for forbidden update flags and stating subagent escalation rules without patching user-authored artefacts.

cases.prompt: Review how eval-system lands in this workspace: summarise `workspace/.zoto/eval-system/config.yml`, relate `manifest.yml` to `manifest.history.yml`, map `workspace/evals/` backends and strategies actually present, eyeball `_runs/` volume against retention defaults, inspect `workspace/package.json` for eval-related scripts, and compare shipping copy under `workspace/plugins/zoto-eval-system/` to any Cursor-local plugin checkout path disclosed in-thread—without rewriting those files.
assertions: Closing guidance inventories which eval-ish scripts (`eval`, `eval:full`, `eval:llm`, `eval:judge`, `eval:gc`, `eval:update`, and related mergers) appear versus ones that are absent from `package.json` when inspected.
The review relates observed `_runs/` folder depth to configured retention semantics wherever those numbers are readable alongside the folders.
Local versus monorepo plugin comparisons rely only on directories the briefing or workspace exposes, omitting fabricated machine-specific paths.
cases.expected_output: A factual walkthrough tying on-disk artefacts to behaviours (retention counts, scaffolded backends, wired scripts), plus a delta narrative between named plugin locations when both are visible to the reviewer.

cases.prompt: Explain, as the engineer, how `/z-eval-update` classifies drift using each `criticalChangeRules` toggle, distinguishes warn-only non-critical deltas from failures when `--check` is used, honours `update.checkExitCodeOnCriticalDrift`, and revisits cached analyser JSON versus live `pnpm run eval:analyse` reruns—with and without `--no-analyser`—during apply cycles.
assertions: The explanation lists each default critical rule identifier and states they behave as active when unspecified in config commentary.
Non-critical drift is portrayed as telemetry appended without tripping `--check`, while configured critical drift aligns with documented exit signalling.
Apply-mode narration covers strategy-specific stamping splits (declarative JSON versus generated TypeScript) while asserting user-authored artefacts stay untouched absent generated markers.
Explanatory prose cites manifest deltas, YAML run summaries, stamped eval artefacts, or process exit meanings the reviewer can corroborate from disk.
cases.expected_output: Operator-facing prose that enumerates drift keys, contrasts warn-only versus critical behaviours, cites exit semantics, and covers regeneration versus cache reuse narratives tied to artefacts on disk.

cases.prompt: Run a read-only partition review on `workspace/evals/`: look for YAML cases marked `_meta.generated: true`, TypeScript or pytest banners claiming generated whole files, and any user-maintained buckets that mistakenly carry generated markers or vice versa. Summarise compliance with the regenerate-only policy.
assertions: Recommendations hinge on artefacts tagged `_meta.generated: true` surviving regeneration while user-authored envelopes remain verbatim.
Violations cite concrete relative paths uncovered during the review rather than hand-wavy claims.
If raw files are withheld from inspection, the agent returns structured `needs_user_input` listing which paths remain unreadable instead of emitting askQuestion from the subagent loop.
cases.expected_output: A grep-oriented audit summarising mismatches plus prioritised remediation that keeps user-authored envelopes pristine.

cases.prompt: Critique deterministic runners for `workspace/evals/static/` and LLM backends under `workspace/evals/llm/`: deterministic ordering, mocking of external IO, fidelity to schemas, `@cursor/sdk` integration risks, and whether coverage maps to hotspots in engine modules referenced by the manifests.
assertions: The critique names which backends (vitest vs pytest vs blended) truly exist beneath `evals/` and ties claims to filenames.
Proposed hygiene improvements cite real files touched by configs or suites rather than abstract praise.
When smoke tests are missing, the critique names concrete engine files that remain uncovered assuming those paths exist.
cases.expected_output: An engineering assessment linking runner configuration files to observable strengths and regressions.

cases.prompt: The team wants the eval-system engineer to pick between deprecating declarative LLM suites entirely and rewriting plugin marketplace-facing docs. Respond from this agent persona: clarify what you refuse, who owns strategic or marketplace edits, and what implementation reviews you remain willing to perform next.
assertions: The reply refuses architecture-only strategy deprecation debates and cites eval-system architect ownership for those judgments.
The reply declines README, CHANGELOG, and marketplace manifest rewrites while deferring those to plugin-manager stewardship.
The reply reaffirms openness to typed engine, hook, `_user-case-guards`, schema parity, drift, manifest, or host-layout reviews within the retained charter.
cases.expected_output: A short refusal that redirects strategy and README churn while offering concrete scoped technical follow-ups.

Classify severities and size recommendations; do not alter plugin sources unless this message explicitly authorises edits.
assertions: Every substantive finding cites a `startLine:endLine:filepath` span in plain text.
Findings classify impact using the blocker through info ladder spelled out for this agent.
The narrative keeps plugin directories and `.zoto/eval-system/` read-only unless edit authorisation appeared in the user message.
cases.expected_output: A markdown audit where each finding cites a line-range code reference, uses the documented severity bands, avoids edits outside authorised scope, and names the downstream owner only when escalation is warranted.

primitive_analysis.summary: Eval cases task the engineer agent with read-only engine audits, schema and contract tracing, host-repo layout reviews, drift semantics, generated-artefact partition checks, test harness critique, and explicit hand-offs away from architecture or plugin-meta work.
